task_completion.description: The draw.io file is created and saved in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io application is open with the filename 'ecommerce_c4_model.drawio' visible in the title bar or the file is visible in the file manager under '~/Diagrams/'.

task_completion.description: The draw.io file contains the three required pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom page tabs in the draw.io interface show exactly three pages named 'System Context', 'Container Diagram', and 'Legend'.

task_completion.description: The 'System Context' page contains a Level 1 C4 diagram with users, the system, and external systems.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagram on the 'System Context' page showing at least 6 shapes, including user personas (Web Customer, Mobile Customer, Back-Office User) and external systems, with labeled relationship arrows.

task_completion.description: The 'Container Diagram' page contains a Level 2 C4 diagram with internal microservices, databases, and the Event Bus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagram on the 'Container Diagram' page showing at least 10 shapes representing microservices (e.g., Catalog.API, Basket.API), databases, and the Event Bus, with protocol-labeled relationship arrows.

task_completion.description: The diagrams use the specified C4 color conventions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Shapes in the diagrams are colored according to the rules: blue for owned elements/microservices, grey for external systems, yellow for user personas, and light blue for databases.

task_completion.description: The completed diagram is exported as a PDF to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'ecommerce_c4_model.pdf' is visible in the '~/Diagrams/' folder in the file manager, or a terminal command shows its existence.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagrams.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io application interface being actively used to place shapes, edit text, and configure colors, rather than just a terminal window running a script.

integrity.description: The agent read the system specification file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the file '~/Desktop/system_spec.txt' open in a text editor or being read via terminal commands (e.g., cat, less).

integrity.description: The PDF was exported using the draw.io application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io 'File -> Export as -> PDF' menu or dialog being used, rather than a command-line conversion tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output diagram path: /home/ga/Diagrams/ecommerce_c4_model.drawio. Output PDF path: /home/ga/Diagrams/ecommerce_c4_model.pdf. Required pages: Exactly 3 pages named 'System Context', 'Container Diagram', and 'Legend'. Minimum shapes for System Context: 6. Minimum shapes for Container Diagram: 10. System name: 'eShopOnContainers'. C4 Color conventions: blue (#1168BD) for owned elements, grey for external systems, yellow for user personas, and light blue for databases.
